subject: ELA
title: Writing & Punctuation
description: Commas, apostrophes, quotation marks, and sentence structure.

questions.q: Where does the apostrophe go? 'The dogs bone was buried.'
questions.explanation: Singular possessive: the bone belonging to one dog = dog's bone.

questions.q: Which sentence uses commas CORRECTLY in a list?
options: I bought apples oranges, and bananas.
I bought apples, oranges, and bananas.
I, bought apples, oranges and bananas.
I bought apples oranges and bananas.
questions.answer: I bought apples, oranges, and bananas.
questions.explanation: Use commas to separate items in a list.

questions.q: Which punctuation mark ends a question?
questions.explanation: A question mark (?) ends a sentence that asks something.

questions.q: What is the TOPIC SENTENCE in: 'Dogs make great pets. They are loyal and fun. Many families enjoy having one.'
questions.explanation: The topic sentence states the main idea — usually the first sentence.
'The bird sang' has a subject (bird) + predicate (sang).

questions.q: Correct the capitalization: 'last summer, my family visited washington, d.c.'
questions.answer: Last summer, my family visited Washington, D.C.
questions.explanation: Sentence-starting words and proper nouns must be capitalized.

questions.q: Which sentence shows CORRECT use of quotation marks?
questions.explanation: Quotation marks go around the exact words someone says.

questions.q: What type of writing tells a story from the writer's own life?
questions.explanation: A personal narrative tells a story from the writer's own experience.

questions.q: A CONCLUDING SENTENCE at the end of a paragraph should...
options: Restate or wrap up the main idea
questions.answer: Restate or wrap up the main idea
questions.explanation: A concluding sentence brings the paragraph to a close by restating the main point.
'But' joins two ideas that contrast each other.
